titleStmt.title: Letter from Róise Ní Dómnaill to Charlie Daly [January 1923]
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Róise Ní Dómnaill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, Co Donegal, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/24/2 Ballybofey, Thursday A cara Thanks for your letter
which I rec’d when on hols. I got
back here about a week ago or
more. I didn’t enjoy my holiday
by any means . Xmas passed very
quietly. If it weren’t for the bad
weather we couldn’t realize it
was that important time of
the year at all. Do you know
Rogers? I think you do. She
wishes to be remembered to yourself
and Ginger. She was home
for a few weeks there hearing a
brother in law of hers dead.
How are you off for cigs? I would
send over some but I’m only writing
this on chance as I don’t know
whether you have been removed
or not: I heard most of the
boys were taken to 7 wier yesterday
I hope you get the daily paper all
right. All around Dungloe
are well and many of them send
their kindest regards. Remember me
to all the boys. Do cara
Roise Ni Domhnaill
Chars. Daly Esq.,
Drumboe Castle,
Stranorlar.
